items.title: Airbnb-backed WeRoad raises $58M to take its group travel platform to the US
items.summary: WeRoad, the Milan-based group travel startup, has raised a $58 million Series C round led by Airbnb as it prepares for its first major expansion outside Europe. The funding brings the company’s total capital raised to roughly $100 million and will finance WeRoad’s push into the U.S., beginning with Austin. The new investment reflects a […]

items.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
items.summary: The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the company sent attorneys.

items.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
items.summary: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

items.title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US
items.summary: Volvo, which is majority owned by China's Geely Holdings, said it can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factory.

items.title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
items.summary: The U.S. government is sitting on dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium. It's hoping startups can find a use for it.

items.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
items.summary: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

items.title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
items.summary: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

items.title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
items.summary: American Airlines said Tuesday it plans to install Starlink on more than 500 Airbus aircraft, the latest carrier win for IPO-bound SpaceX.

items.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
items.summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

items.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
items.summary: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

items.title: Dutch government blocks US company from acquisition, citing ‘risk to public interest’
items.summary: The move to block the acquisition of the cloud company that hosts the Dutch digital ID service comes as Europe continues to reduce its reliance on U.S. technology.

items.title: Ghost hackers: the cybersecurity mystery that nobody has solved
items.summary: A shadowy group that stole and dumped the NSA’s most powerful hacking tools still has implications for how companies think about digital risk today.

items.title: Iranian hackers blamed for breach of Los Angeles transit system that took weeks to recover
items.summary: An Israeli cybersecurity firm said Iran’s government is behind Ababil of Minab, a fake hacktivist persona that has claimed a series of data breaches after the start of the war in Iran.

items.title: Ferrari’s first EV is not for you
items.summary: The Ferrari Luce seems to be more aimed at regulatory compliance and China, putting a lot of pressure on the Jony Ive-designed EV.

items.title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
items.summary: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies.

items.title: Spotify now lets you stream narrated magazine articles, too
items.summary: The streaming service said that, starting today, there will be over 650 long-form magazine articles (available in English only) from publications like Rolling Stone, The Atlantic, Vogue, Variety, Billboard, Vibe, GQ, Wired, Vanity Fair, and Pitchfork.

items.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
items.summary: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.

items.title: 7-Eleven data breach affects over 185,000 people’s personal data
items.summary: The data breach included names, dates of birth, postal addresses, and Social Security numbers, according to a state government listing.

items.title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
items.summary: Stord offers a network of physical warehouses and inventory management software for e-commerce. It bills itself as a sort of anti-Amazon, giving brands "the speed to compete" while still owning their customer relationships.

items.title: What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work
items.summary: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
